scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a cultivation closeout only when licence, site, plant, harvest, package, testing, and transfer evidence were explicit and internally consistent.
Missing jurisdiction, licence, system-of-record, and authority-map context kept the work blocked and produced an exception summary rather than a generic farming recommendation.
Incomplete plant-tag, environmental, and disposal records correctly moved the work into hold or rework posture with named artifact gaps.
Failed or pending testing posture reversed the prior transfer-ready state and routed the work into exception handling instead of unsupported completion.
Water-source, pesticide, and permit authority requests were rejected cleanly and routed to the proper compliance, environmental, and legal owners.
Completion was refused until package UID, laboratory disposition, and transfer-manifest evidence existed, keeping lifecycle transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Inspection and diversion concerns were packaged for the correct compliance and security path rather than being resolved inside cultivation operations.
Realistic cultivation terminology and artifact shapes produced an industry-specific execution summary instead of generic agricultural output.
The lane rejected a generic cultivation framing until cannabis-specific jurisdiction, tracking, testing, and authority context were explicit.
Tenant-specific adaptation preserved the public baseline while separating California Metrc and Canadian CTLS handling instead of flattening them into one rule path.
Low-confidence handling requested the actual track-and-trace, testing, and authority-owner records and escalated instead of guessing after an environmental excursion.
Conflicting public guidance, tenant SOPs, and regulator instructions correctly triggered source-conflict escalation instead of confident synthesis.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against a 0.92 minimum threshold for cultivation execution, exception handling, completion evidence, and cannabis-specific boundary safety.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities.
